Validation rule: Format: 2-letter country code + up to 15 alphanumeric chars

To identify declarant, required by law

Purpose: Identifies the internal person/team responsible for the data.
Validation rule: Non-empty; must be a valid internal contact

Purpose: To identify assigned code for the import
Used to determine whether the product may be within CBAM scope.
Validation rule: Must be exactly 8 digits; must fall within CBAM Annex I codes
Notes/assumptions: One row per one CN Code +supplier + country

Purpose: Full product description matching the CN code classification
Example value: Semi-finished iron or non-alloy steel (carbon <0.25%) with a rectangular cross-section and a thickness not exceeding 130 mm

Purpose: Helps classify the product under CBAM-relevant categories.
Simple Goods (no precursors) or Complex Goods (contains CBAM precursors)
Net mass of imported goods in metric tonnes for the reporting/analysing period period
Indicates the period the data belongs to.
Notes/assumptions: Reporting period - 1 year, analysing period could be any period

Purpose: Country of production, required for CBAM scope determination.
Validation rule: Must match ISO 3166-1, for CBAM scope must be outside EU + exemption countries from the regulation

Purpose: Reference number of the customs import declaration
Links the product to the supplier providing or producing the goods.
Captures additional context or known data limitations.
